tweets.text:
The defining losses of this cycle trace back to token structures.

Token unlocks erased a median 7% against BTC across 400+ tracked events, and holders had no claim on the revenue their protocols earned.

The revenue side changed once protocols started routing fees back to the token. A basket of the top ten protocols weighted by revenue returned +30% from January 2025 while BTC, ETH, and SOL all fell.

The next cycle will be built on fewer tokens that reward their holders.

tweets.text:
AI models get the most attention, but the real moat could be the harness.

Earlier this year, a Meta and Harvard team beat Anthropic at SWE-bench Pro with a cheaper model. Their custom harness wrapped around Sonnet 4.5 scored 52.7%, while Anthropic's own scaffold running the more expensive Opus 4.5 trailed at 52.0%.

With Hermes, the agent's memory of you lives in files on your machine. Your conversations go into a local SQLite database. Honcho runs dialectic reasoning over your past conversations to build a persistent model of how you work.

The agent writes new skills from its own successful runs. Your agent becomes more useful over time as these skill files build up.

Models will be replaced over time, but your harness stays with you.

tweets.text:
This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets it" and it will just go, and it's never felt this tempting to stop looking at the code at all (but don't do this in prod!). The model still has quirks that people will run into and the safeguards are configured to be a little too trigger happy for launch, which can hopefully be tuned over time.

I feel a lot of things changing as working software increasingly comes out on a tap. The Jevon's paradox kicks in and I feel my own demand for software growing substantially. You can ask for anything - explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps (e.g. a full wandb that is hyper-specific just for your project), you can 10X your test suite, auto-optimize code, run giant research projects with custom HTML for the results, anything! "Free your mind" (Matrix ref). Really looking forward to all the things people build!

hell yeah anthropic just released 2 of their most powerful models claude fable 5 and claude mythos 5. they're officially #1 and less than half the cost of mythos-preview:

-> claude fable = restricted version of mythos for public use

-> mythos 5 = anthropic's unrestricted model for private use only (government and glasswing partners) takes the crown for #1 model by a wide margin

-> fable's unbelievable at coding. Stripe used it to accelerate a coding project from 2 months to days.

-> vision-mode turns fable into a frontier model that can see everything you can. it beats pokemon fire red on minimal settings and produces visually stunning images

-> cost is only 2x opus 4.8. surprising and way more accessible for mass use

-> drug-design: mythos-5 accelerated parts of the protein design process by 10x (zero human help)

-> fable has strict safe-guards in place, if it suspects you're asking a hazardous prompt it re-routes to opus 4.8

important to note: access is limited to june 22nd at which it moves to a usage-based model due to limited compute.
